comment.body: It would be interesting to execute a case study of the local top in January of 2022. Was the market well hedged before that drop off? When you take XLK, XLY, XLC (growth tech) and you divide it by XLP, XLV, XLU, XLRE (defensive/cyclical) the was a big divergence, where this ratio made a lower high while SPX made an ATH. Same is occurring today. Big divergence with that ratio from mid December 24’ to the ATH just struck.

comment.body: Also, if you want the FED to cut rates, wouldn’t the “powers that be” be able to force Powell’s hand by tanking the market? Moreover, Trump has always talked about how well the market is doing under his presidency. So clearly it is important to him. What better way to force him to change course, to stop the cutting government spending, than tanking the market? The FED rarely ever cuts in strength. It has always cut when it’s hand is force by a market temper tantrum

comment.body: Indeed. And there are a lot of secular tailwinds for the US markets. The boomers are retiring and a lot of them will gradually sell their portfolios down. If there is going to be mass layoffs younger people might have to do the same to be able to survive. A lot of these portfolios (especially of the older generation) are sitting at ATHs. Market always went up, dips were shallow. Any bigger pullback that is not immediately retraced will create some more severe selling

comment.body: the market i think is unlikely to crash because of the debt ceiling. The debt ceiling has been reached so the Treasury cannot drain money from the economy by issuing bonds to finance its spending (thei is a zero sum game), so they have to spend money out of the TGA (TREASURY GENNERAL ACCOUNT) ...Government savings. That has about $800Bill in it,  enough to finance 4 months deficit. this money is usually stagnant in the economy sitting earning interest. It will now be spent into the economy...this is like QE without being QE...an extra $150B of liquidity every month until debt ceiling is raised.....then the bonds will be issued as normal but extra will have to be sold to refill the TGA.   tHE OTHER EFFECT WHEN NO BONDS ARE BEING SOLD IS IT HOLDS INTEREST RATES DOWN, AS THERE IS DEMAND FOR us DEBT BUT LIMITED SUPPLY WHEN DEBT CEILING IS IN PLACE.... so ONCE DEBT CEILING IS RAISED WE ARE MUCH MORE LIKELY TO SEE A CRASH IN MARKETS

It will now be spent into the economy...this is like QE without being QE...an extra $150B of liquidity every month until debt ceiling is raised.....then the bonds will be issued as normal but extra will have to be sold to refill the TGA.   tHE OTHER EFFECT WHEN NO BONDS ARE BEING SOLD IS IT HOLDS INTEREST RATES DOWN, AS THERE IS DEMAND FOR us DEBT BUT LIMITED SUPPLY WHEN DEBT CEILING IS IN PLACE.... so ONCE DEBT CEILING IS RAISED WE ARE MUCH MORE LIKELY TO SEE A CRASH IN MARKETS

comment.body:
Another thing to consider, if the economy/market performs poorly, and we get 4 years of chop/volatility under Trump, that would certainly be good for “the left”. And no side is perfect, but there certainly is a power struggle in the West between to current world order, which is being implemented and carried out by the left, and whatever this conservative/Maga/populist movement is of the right.

And there is no doubt that the power structure Trump is trying to blow up is not going down without pulling out all the stops.

And Trump certainly didn’t help himself by promising the moon, the stars, and the greatest economy in the history of the world. If he called their bluff, like Eminem in 8 Mile, and prepared the American people saying there would be a struggle to implement his political vision, then that’s a different story. So you have to think his opposition, which is immensely powerful, is going to call his bluff.

And given the fact Trump is already pushing out the idea of $5K stimulus checks, I have a tough time believing he’s going to stay his current course and say “f$ck, let’s blow this sucker up!”. Because that would be political suicide, and “the left” would step right back into power.

comment.body:
Yes he would lose credibility. When he was campaigning, I don’t think he understood that he was constantly saying things that were diametrically opposed to each other. If you are going to drastically cut government spending, that will inevitably create downward pressure on asset prices and the economy. Yet, he was promising the strongest economy in the history of the world and has always referenced how the stock market was the strongest under his administration. He’s also, multiple times, been a champion of negative interest rates and has called for them during his last administration. So he’s got a lot of hypocrisy in his proposed policy vision. 

So my point is, the “powers that be” have a great opportunity to force his hand. And out of a necessity of survival, the “less of two evils” is for him to continue the “business as usual”, because holding true to decreasing government spending, on shoring jobs and increasing wages, would be too painful for the American people to absorb. Especially after the promises Trump has made.

And out of a necessity of survival, the “less of two evils” is for him to continue the “business as usual”, because holding true to decreasing government spending, on shoring jobs and increasing wages, would be too painful for the American people to absorb. Especially after the promises Trump has made.

comment.body:
Been reading Alma’s latest post a few times. Is this the gist you all are getting? Please let me know where I’m missing things. :::: 

Big players are hedging short term against large drops, while mid term they are shorting the skew (showing no real fear).  Because they do this while pushing bear narrative in media, they are setting the stage for a short term bear trap. After this current bear trap vol crushes spot up, insiders will distribute at ATH’s (maybe no new ATH if trendline isn’t broken, just repetitive, sell off, melt up, hence dangerous to trade long term right now).  

After this stage eventually we will see intentional sell off that will cause weak hands to panic sell, then the cash rich insiders will repurchase lowering their cost basis. 

Why? Because they want government sponsored capitalism running full steam again. A fed put ensuring victory in the upcoming economic war with china, and they get richer all the while.

comment.body: Well it true they will start issuing bonds but it shouldn’t be inflationary because they are borrowing money which is already in the economy. However they will need to issue twice as many bonds to refill the TGA…. Likely to push rates higher. This all happened under Yellen but she drained the reverse repo where $2T was sitting stagnant. She issued bills 80% and coupons 20%….bills under 1 yr debt. The reason she did this was to goose the economy because banks can buy bills because they don’t effect there balance sheets as they are held less than 1 yr…so they can create money to buy the bills….inflationary. For Trump reverse repo is empty so his borrowings have to come from more normal sources
